(function () {
  try {
    if (window.__mintlifyInitialSidebarScrollDone) return;
    window.__mintlifyInitialSidebarScrollDone = true;

    var path = (window.location.pathname || '/').split('#')[0].split('?')[0];
    if (path.endsWith('/index')) path = path.slice(0, -6);
    else if (path === 'index') path = '';

    var candidates = [];
    if (path) candidates.push(path);
    if (path.startsWith('/')) candidates.push(path.slice(1));
    else candidates.push('/' + path);

    var item = null;
    for (var i = 0; i < candidates.length && !item; i++) {
      var matches = document.querySelectorAll('[id="' + candidates[i].replace(/"/g, '\\"') + '"]');
      for (var j = 0; j < matches.length; j++) {
        if (matches[j].closest('#sidebar, #sidebar-content')) {
          item = matches[j];
          break;
        }
      }
    }
    if (!item) return;

    var parent = item.parentElement;
    while (parent) {
      var style = getComputedStyle(parent);
      if (style.overflowY === 'auto' || style.overflowY === 'scroll') break;
      parent = parent.parentElement;
    }
    if (!parent) return;

    var parentRect = parent.getBoundingClientRect();
    var itemRect = item.getBoundingClientRect();
    if (itemRect.top >= parentRect.top && itemRect.bottom <= parentRect.bottom) return;

    var itemTopRelative = itemRect.top - parentRect.top + parent.scrollTop;
    parent.scrollTop = itemTopRelative - parentRect.height / 2 + itemRect.height / 2;
  } catch (e) {}
})();

  }
  .uc-hero-inner {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    gap: 24px;
    padding: 0 16px;
  }
  .uc-hero-title {
    font-size: 28px;
  }
  .uc-hero-meta {
    flex-direction: row;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 20px;
  }
}
@media (max-width: 480px) {
  .uc-hero-title {
    font-size: 24px;
  }
  .uc-hero-desc {
    font-size: 14px;
  }
  .uc-hero-meta {
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 16px;
  }
}

/* Try in Devin button in hero section — larger standalone style */
.uc-hero .try-in-devin-btn {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 10px 20px;
  background: #317CFF;
  color: #fff !important;
  border-radius: 8px;
  font-size: 14px;
  font-weight: 500;
  text-decoration: none !important;
  transition: background 0.2s;
  border: none;
  cursor: pointer;
  margin-top: 24px;
  opacity: 1;
}
.uc-hero .try-in-devin-btn:hover {
  background: #2968D9;
  color: #fff !important;
}
.uc-hero .try-in-devin-logo {
  width: 20px;
  height: 20px;
}

/* ===== Federal docs =====
   The Federal section is a standalone area that is intentionally NOT linked from
   the commercial docs. It lives as a normal tab so its pages keep a full sidebar;
   we simply hide the Federal tab from the top navbar so commercial readers never
   see it. Federal users reach the section by direct URL and navigate via the
   sidebar.

   Mintlify renders each top tab as an <a class="nav-tabs-item" href="..."> whose
   href points at the tab's first page, so we hide the anchor whose href is in the
   federal section. */
a.nav-tabs-item[href*="/federal"] {
  display: none !important;
}

Releases
Changelog
Copy page
Release notes for Devin Desktop (Windsurf).
Copy page
​
v3.4.27
July 7, 2026
Fixes issues with diff viewing in autonomous mode.
Download 3.4.27
​
v3.4.22
July 4, 2026
Devin Desktop
Added “New session in space” to the session kebab menu.
Devin Cloud sessions now auto-reconnect when the network returns.
Images can now be copied from chat via the context menu.
New users now default to agent mode.
The agent sidebar now stays visible when sending problems or explain-and-fix to Cascade in agent window mode.
Fixed branch checkout silently failing to open a worktree.
Very large sessions no longer crash the window when reading or writing the session event cache.
The “Scroll to top” button in long sessions now has a solid background so it stays visible in dark themes.
Orphaned Devin ACP agent processes are now detected and cleaned up on startup, including on Windows.
Fixed analytics connection failures under TLS-intercepting proxies.
Devin Local
Edits produced in autonomous mode now produce reviewable diffs.
Added an
/mcp
slash command with a live MCP server status panel.
ACU usage is now shown in the
/usage
command.
Skill
permissions:
frontmatter now applies to auto-approvals.
Enterprise login policies are now enforced in the CLI.
Added a
sandbox.excluded
allow/ask/deny config (user and team settings) to run specific commands outside the sandbox; excluded commands also skip the sandbox proxy environment.
Fixed command approval parsing for PowerShell
$variable
assignment prefixes.
Download 3.4.22
​
v3.3.18
June 23, 2026
Devin Desktop
Devin ACU usage is now displayed in the client.
Fixed settings and extensions migration for Windows system-wide installs.
CLI and Devin Local
On Windows,
bash
now resolves to Git Bash instead of the WSL launcher stub.
Subagents can now be configured with a default model.
The MCP registry cache is now warmed during startup, so MCP servers are ready sooner.
Injected context is no longer included in auto-generated session titles.
Fixed agent messages over-merging in Claude ACP sessions.
Added an
attribution
option to the Devin Local
config file
; set it to
false
to suppress Devin mentions in commit messages.
Download 3.3.18

mode-toggle shortcut from the empty editor welcome panel.

The 3.1 series builds on the Devin Desktop rebrand with a smoother Agent/Editor experience, Devin Local improvements, and continued polish.
Agent and Editor modes
Added the Agent/Editor switch to the collapsed-sidebar titlebar, and unified the search icon to open agent search.
Kept the sidebar toggle in place when opening and closing the drawer.
Smoothed switching between Agent and Editor modes so auxiliary windows no longer close and restore.
Devin Local
Renamed the “Devin CLI” settings section to “Devin Local”.
Added a notification when Devin Local agent sign-in fails.
Updated the bundled Devin Local agent to
v2026.5.26-8
.

.devinignore
support alongside
.windsurfignore
and
.codeiumignore
.
Settings and MCP marketplace pages now scroll across the full panel width and keep content centered on wide panels.

v3.0.21
June 4, 2026
Added a new command for Devin Desktop to rerun the migration from Windsurf. This will reset Devin Desktop settings and give you a second chance to import extensions and other settings from Windsurf. The “Reset migration from Windsurf” command can be found in the command palette.
If you are logged out of your account after migrating from Windsurf, we recommend opening the command palette via ctrl+shift+p (cmd+shift+p on macOS) and running the reset migration command.

Bug fixes and improvements
​
General
Increased remote server startup timeout from 2.5s to 6s.
​
Devin Local
This release updates the bundled Devin Local agent to 2026.5.26. See the
changelog
for the full list of changes.
Devin Local is now aware of the files you have open in the editor as part of its context.
When prompted for an MCP tool permission in Devin Local, two additional server-level options are now offered: approve all tools on the server for the current session, or permanently.
Repaired hooks for Devin Local to allow blocking user prompts.
Improved plan mode in Devin Local to work in the OS sandbox.
“Always Allow” permission grants in Devin Local now persist across sessions.
Image attachments in Devin Local now show the correct warning when the selected model does not support images.

v2.1.29

​
Devin for Terminal
Devin is now available
for Terminal
. All Windsurf users can use this new CLI agent with your existing subscription.
Runs on your machine
— Optimized for interactive work, with full access to your codebase, tools, and environment.
Hand off to the cloud
— Seamless hand off to Devin in the cloud, with its own VM, testing, video recordings, autofix and more. Come back to a finished PR.
Multi-model
— All of your favorite frontier models in one place, including Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, and SWE-1.6.
Fast
— Written in Rust and so performant that the binary can run on an original VT100.
​
Devin Agent in Windsurf
You can also enable the new Devin Local agent in Windsurf. This is the same agent harness used on the terminal and sessions can be accessed from both Windsurf and the CLI.
In our testing, it’s up to 30% more token-efficient than the existing Cascade agent.
​
Additional Changes
Improved search subtitle layout during streaming
Fixed file drag-and-drop in agent window Cascade tabs
Fixed Go to Line/Column keybinding on Windows/Linux (Ctrl+Shift+G)
Added support for server-driven extension deny lists
Stability and performance improvements

Windsurf 2.0
Read the full announcement
here
.
​
Devin in Windsurf
Devin cloud agent available directly inside Windsurf, included with every self-serve plan
Delegate tasks from a local session to Devin with one click; Devin runs on its own VM
Review Devin changes and test results without leaving the editor
Billing is based on your existing quota and extra usage, with up to 50 USD in extra usage added for launching your first Devin Cloud session.
Note:
Access to Devin Cloud is rolling out gradually. If you don’t see it yet, try logging out of the website and IDE then logging back in.
Devin Cloud is disabled by default for enterprise accounts. Enterprise admins should enable Devin access in their organization settings if they have already purchased Cognition Platform.
​
Agent Command Center
New Kanban-style view showing all local and cloud agent sessions, organized by status
Group agent sessions, PRs, files, and context into task-level Spaces
Switch between Spaces to switch between tasks
​
Additional Changes
Refined Windsurf Browser with toolbar integration and Cascade tool for reading page contents
Sped up initial load times for the Cascade sidebar
Improved .gitignore and .codeiumignore handling across the product
Stability improvements for remote extensions (WSL, SSH, Dev Containers)
Performance improvements for typing in large active diff zones

v1.9600.38
April 6, 2026
​
Introducing Adaptive
We’ve made several model packaging changes, with more info
here
.
​
Adaptive Model Router
A new
Adaptive
model option is now available in the model picker. Adaptive intelligently selects the best model for each task, helping you make your quota last longer by avoiding overuse of premium models.
Availability
: Now available to all self-serve users on Pro, Max, and Teams plans.
Dynamic model selection
- Automatically chooses the right underlying model for your task while drawing down quota at a fixed per-token rate.
Extra usage promo
- Beyond your quota, extra usage is offered at 0.50 USD per 1M input tokens, 2.00 USD per 1M output tokens, and 0.10 USD per 1M cache read tokens for the next 2 weeks.
​
Updated Model Picker with Pricing Context
The model picker now shows token pricing information directly, so you can see the exact rate extra usage is billed at.
Token pricing display
- Per-model input, output, and cache read token rates visible in the picker.
Prompt cache timer
- A new prompt cache timer is integrated into the context window indicator to help you track caching status.
Token counts in response cards
- Response cards after messages now include token counts so you can understand exactly how each message cost was calculated.
Download 1.9600.38

v1.9544.24
January 30, 2026
​
Wave 14: Arena Mode
Arena Mode brings side-by-side model comparison directly into your IDE, plus Plan Mode for smarter task planning.
​
Arena Mode
Run two Cascade agents side-by-side with hidden model identities and vote on which performs better. Arena Mode lets you discover which models actually work best for
your
workflow, codebase, and tasks—not just what benchmarks or influencers say.
Battle Groups
: Choose specific models to compare or let Windsurf randomly select from curated groups like “fast models” vs “smart models”
Personal & Global Leaderboards
: Your votes contribute to both a personal leaderboard (your preferences) and a global one (across all Windsurf users)
Sync or Branch
: Send followup prompts to both agents simultaneously, or branch and explore different paths individually
To get started, select the new
Arena
tab in the model picker. All battle groups are free for the first week for paid users.
​
Plan Mode
Plan Mode is a new Cascade mode alongside Code and Ask. Use it to create detailed implementation plans before diving into code.
Pro tip
: Type
megaplan
in the Cascade input box to trigger an advanced form that asks clarifying questions to create a more aligned, comprehensive plan.
​
Bug Fixes and Improvements
Admins can now set a default model that applies to all team members when they first open Windsurf
Various bug fixes and performance improvements
Download 1.9544.24

Gemini 3 Flash is now available for all users. This model combines Gemini 3 Pro-grade reasoning with Flash-level speed and efficiency, making it ideal for agentic workflows and coding tasks.
Blazing Fast Responses
: Experience near-instant feedback with 3x faster performance than previous generations, perfect for iterative development compared to Gemini 3 Pro

: Outperforms even Pro-tier models on key coding benchmarks (78% on SWE-bench Verified), providing more accurate code generation and debugging

v1.13.3
December 24, 2025
​
Wave 13: Merry Shipmas
Wave 13 brings first-class support for parallel, multi-agent sessions in Windsurf, along with Git worktrees, side-by-side Cascade panes, and a dedicated terminal profile for more reliable agent execution.
​
SWE-1.5 Free
Our near-frontier model, SWE-1.5, is now available for free to all users for the next 3 months. SWE-1.5 Free has the full intelligence of SWE-1.5, with the same coding performance on SWE-Bench-Pro, but delivered at standard throughput speeds. The original variant of SWE-1.5 hosted on Cerebras will continue to be available for paid users. SWE-1.5 Free will replace SWE-1 as the default model in Windsurf starting today.
​
Git Worktree Support
Windsurf now supports Git worktrees, letting you spawn multiple Cascade sessions in the same repository without conflicts. Git worktrees check out different branches into separate directories while sharing the same Git history.
​
Multi-Cascade Panes & Tabs
You can already run multiple Cascade sessions in Windsurf at the same time. Now, you can view and interact with them in separate panes and tabs within the same window. This lets you monitor progress and compare outputs of sessions side-by-side, or even turn Windsurf into a big Cascade dashboard.
​
Cascade Dedicated Terminal (Beta)
Windsurf introduces a new approach for letting agents execute terminal commands. Instead of your default shell, Cascade will now run commands in a dedicated zsh shell specifically configured for reliability. The Cascade Dedicated Terminal can use the environment variables you set in your .zshrc configuration and is interactive, which means you can answer any prompts from shell scripts without having to break your flow. This should improve the reliability and speed of shell commands, especially for users with complicated prompts (e.g., powerlevel10k).
In this version, the Cascade Dedicated Terminal will be opt-in for Windsurf Stable on macOS. If you are experiencing issues with the older legacy terminal, we recommend switching to the new terminal early. We expect to make this feature the default in the future, while maintaining the legacy terminal extension for backwards compatibility purposes. You can opt-in in the Windsurf User Settings -> Disable Windsurf Legacy Terminal Profile.
​
Context Window Indicator
When a model’s context window grows too long, earlier context can be dropped without warning and performance can degrade. Cascade already extends the window by occasionally summarizing messages and clearing history. This release adds a visual indicator to see how much of your context window is currently in use, helping you anticipate limits and decide when to start a new session.
​
Cascade Hooks
Execute custom commands at key points during Cascade’s workflow, including on model response for auditing purposes.
​
System-level Rules & Workflows

v1.8.2

Cascade Customization
​
Cascade UX Improvements
Redesigned Model Selector
Continue button when reaching individual tool call limit
Opening conversations will now open the associated workspace
Hunk accept/reject widget now has a compact mode to cover less code
Improvements to commit message generation quality
Commit message generation reads from global rules as context
Ability to edit proposed terminal command
​
Custom Workflows
You can create “workflows”, saved prompts that Cascade can follow
Workflows can be invoked via slash command
Cascade can help create and edit workflows
Workflow files are saved in the workspace, under .windsurf/workflows
​
File-Based Rules
You can create granular rules files that are always on, @mention-able, requested by Cascade, or attached to file globs
Rules files are saved in the workspace, under .windsurf/rules
​
Simultaneous Cascades
Allow Cascade to keep running when switching to another conversation
Add support for switching between conversations via a dropdown menu or keyboard shortcuts
​
Cascade Plugins
New panel in Cascade for managing MCP Servers
Easier one-click uninstall and install
Easier search
MCP now has MCP resources and multimodel responses
More MCP Server options coming soon

Teams Features
​
Teams: Windsurf Reviews
Team admins can install a Github app for code review and PR title/description edits

Available to Teams and Enterprise SaaS

Available to Teams and Enterprise SaaS

Updated & Simplified Pricing
​
We’re getting rid of Flow Action Credits
We’re simplifying our pricing model by removing Flow Action Credits

Add-on credits are available for purchase
Auto-top off (with max limits) can be enabled via profile

Existing Plans

Pricing page

April 2, 2025
​
Deploys
​
Deploys (Beta)
Deploy your application with one prompt to Netlify under a windsurf.build domain
Claim your application’s URL via Netlify
Once claimed, continue deploying to the same project as you make updates
To deploy a new site or change your subdomain, just ask Cascade to deploy to a new subdomain
Available to all users for all tiers, with more for paid plans
​
Commit Message Generation (Beta)
Generate commit messages with a click in the Source Control Panel
Available to users on paid plans with no additional credit cost per use
​
Improvements to Memories
New memories tab in Cascade
New ability to edit Cascade’s generated memories, including the memory’s title, content and tags
New ability to search Cascade’s generated memories
User setting toggle for Auto-Generate Memories
When enabled, Cascade will autonomously generate memories to remember important context
When disabled, Cascade will only create memories when you explicitly ask in your prompt
​
Improvements to Long Conversations
Introduced Cascade table of contents of all past user messages, which appears on conversation scroll
Table of contents enables the ability to revert or scroll to any past message
Improved performance when interacting with long conversations
​
Improvements to Windsurf Tab
Jupyter Notebook Support for Windsurf Tab
Additional context signals for Windsurf Tab, including in-IDE search
​
New Mac Icons
Two new application icons (Retro and Pixel Surf) are available for users on paid plans
​
Misc
Cascade new conversation screen now has a new toolbar for tools like MCP, Preview and Deployments.
Cascade now supports SSE MCP servers in the JSON configuration
Fixed “Open Cascade on Reload” setting so Cascade will be closed upon opening a new window when setting is disabled

self.__next_f.push([1,"/**\n * Checks that all the links in the docs are valid. Handrolled because the Mintlify link checker has too many false positives. Run `npm run check` or `node check_links.js` to run this script.\n *\n * Right now, we only verify that internal links (links that point to other docs pages) are valid.\n *\n * Including \"lint:no-link-check\" or \"lint:no-link-check-next-line\" anywhere in a line will disable link checking for that line or the subsequent line, respetively.\n *\n * We currently don't account for redirects. Our internal links shouldn't need a layer of redirection anyways, and we should instead just update them if we move pages around.\n *\n * Feel free to ping me (Ryan) if this script stops working.\n */\n\nconst fs = require(\"node:fs\");\nconst path = require(\"node:path\");\nconst process = require(\"node:process\");\nconst config = require(\"./docs.json\");\n\nconst DISABLE_LINK_CHECK = \"lint:no-link-check\";\nconst DISABLE_LINK_CHECK_NEXT_LINE = \"lint:no-link-check-next-line\";\n\nfunction extractPages(pages) {\n  const result = [];\n  for (const page of pages) {\n    if (typeof page === 'string') {\n      result.push(page);\n    } else if (page \u0026\u0026 typeof page === 'object' \u0026\u0026 page.pages) {\n      result.push(...extractPages(page.pages));\n    }\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nconst pages = config.navigation.languages.flatMap((lang) =\u003e\n  lang.tabs.flatMap((tab) =\u003e \n    tab.groups.flatMap((group) =\u003e extractPages(group.pages))\n  )\n);\n\nfunction isBadLink(pagePath, link) {\n  const url = new URL(link, `https://docs.devin.ai/${pagePath}`);\n\n  if (link.includes(\"docs.devin.ai\")) {\n    return { error: \"Links shouldn't include docs.devin.ai\" };\n  }\n  if (url.hostname !== \"docs.devin.ai\") {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return (\n    !fs.existsSync(path.join(__dirname, url.pathname)) \u0026\u0026\n    !fs.existsSync(path.join(__dirname, url.pathname + \".mdx\"))\n  );\n}\n\nfunction scrapeLinks(pageMdx) {\n  const linkRegex = /\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)/g;\n  const links = [];\n  let match;\n\n  const lines = pageMdx.split(\"\\n\");\n  for (let i = 0; i \u003c lines.length; i++) {\n    if (lines[i].includes(DISABLE_LINK_CHECK) || (i \u0026\u0026 lines[i - 1].includes(DISABLE_LINK_CHECK_NEXT_LINE))) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    while ((match = linkRegex.exec(lines[i])) !== null) {\n      links.push(match[2]);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return links;\n}\n\nfunction readPage(page) {\n  return fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, page + \".mdx\"), \"utf8\");\n}\n\nlet hasErrors = false;\n\nfor (const page of pages) {\n  const pageMdx = readPage(page);\n  const links = scrapeLinks(pageMdx);\n\n  const badLinks = links.map((link) =\u003e ({link, error: isBadLink(page, link)})).filter(({error}) =\u003e error);\n\n  if (badLinks.length \u003e 0) {\n    console.log(page + \".mdx\");\n    for (const {link, error} of badLinks) {\n      if (Object.hasOwn(error, \"error\")) {\n        console.log(`  ${link}: ${error.error}`);\n      } else {\n        console.log(`  ${link}`);\n      }\n    }\n    console.log();\n    hasErrors = true;\n  }\n}\n\nif (hasErrors) {\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n"])

self.__next_f.push([1,"(function() {\n  var activeCat = 'All';\n  var activeFeatures = [];\n\n  function applyFilters() {\n    var cards = document.querySelectorAll('.gallery-card');\n    if (!cards.length) return;\n    var visible = 0;\n    cards.forEach(function(card) {\n      var cat = card.getAttribute('data-category') || '';\n      var feats = card.getAttribute('data-features') || '';\n      var catMatch = activeCat === 'All' || cat === activeCat;\n      var featMatch = activeFeatures.length === 0 || activeFeatures.some(function(f) {\n        return feats.split(',').indexOf(f) !== -1;\n      });\n      if (catMatch \u0026\u0026 featMatch) {\n        card.style.display = '';\n        visible++;\n      } else {\n        card.style.display = 'none';\n      }\n    });\n    var empty = document.getElementById('gallery-empty');\n    if (empty) {\n      empty.style.display = visible === 0 ? 'block' : 'none';\n      empty.classList.toggle('hidden', visible \u003e 0);\n    }\n  }\n\n  function init() {\n    document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {\n      var chip = e.target.closest('.gallery-chip');\n      if (chip) {\n        e.preventDefault();\n        var clickedCat = chip.getAttribute('data-category') || 'All';\n        if (clickedCat === activeCat \u0026\u0026 clickedCat !== 'All') {\n          activeCat = 'All';\n          document.querySelectorAll('.gallery-chip').forEach(function(c) {\n            c.classList.remove('active');\n          });\n          var allChip = document.querySelector('.gallery-chip[data-category=\"All\"]');\n          if (allChip) allChip.classList.add('active');\n        } else {\n          activeCat = clickedCat;\n          document.querySelectorAll('.gallery-chip').forEach(function(c) {\n            c.classList.remove('active');\n          });\n          chip.classList.add('active');\n        }\n        applyFilters();\n        return;\n      }\n      var fchip = e.target.closest('.gallery-feature-chip');\n      if (fchip) {\n        e.preventDefault();\n        var feat = fchip.getAttribute('data-feature');\n        var idx = activeFeatures.indexOf(feat);\n        if (idx === -1) {\n          activeFeatures.push(feat);\n          fchip.classList.add('active');\n        } else {\n          activeFeatures.splice(idx, 1);\n          fchip.classList.remove('active');\n        }\n        applyFilters();\n      }\n    });\n  }\n\n  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {\n    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);\n  } else {\n    init();\n  }\n\n})();\n"])

self.__next_f.push([1,"/**\n * Generates the Devin Desktop (Windsurf) changelog and releases pages from the\n * public release feeds.\n *\n * It fetches the list of available versions from the updater's `/api/versions`\n * endpoint for each release channel (stable and next) and emits two Mintlify\n * changelog pages (https://www.mintlify.com/docs/create/changelogs) per channel,\n * each with one `\u003cUpdate\u003e` block per version:\n *\n *   - changelog page: release notes (text) plus a collapsible per-OS download\n *     block per version, wrapped in `\u003cAccordion title=\"Download \u003cversion\u003e\"\u003e`.\n *     The newest version additionally gets an open-by-default accordion so it\n *     stands out. When a note exists at\n *     `desktop/releases/\u003cchannel\u003e/\u003cversion\u003e.md` it is imported (relative path)\n *     and rendered; otherwise a placeholder line is used.\n *   - releases page: per-OS download buttons only, rendered by the `\u003cRelease\u003e`\n *     component fed the download URLs from the API manifest.\n *\n * It also emits a changelog page for the Windsurf JetBrains plugin pre-release\n * channel. The JetBrains pre-release\n * source can be overridden with JETBRAINS_NEXT_INDEX_URL, or read from a local\n * checkout of exafunction.github.io via JETBRAINS_NEXT_SOURCE_DIR (pointing at\n * its `public/changelog` directory).\n */\n\nconst fs = require(\"node:fs\");\nconst path = require(\"node:path\");\nconst process = require(\"node:process\");\n\nconst ROOT = path.join(__dirname, \"..\");\nconst DESKTOP_DIR = path.join(ROOT, \"desktop\");\nconst PLUGINS_DIR = path.join(ROOT, \"windsurf\", \"plugins\");\n\n// Import for the \u003cRelease\u003e component. It gives early access to new features and releases more frequently than the stable build, but also may have bugs.\",\n  },\n];\n\n// Windsurf JetBrains plugin changelogs. When JETBRAINS_SOURCE_DIR is set\n// it is used as the `public/changelog` directory for both channels.\nconst JETBRAINS_SOURCE_DIR =\n  process.env.JETBRAINS_SOURCE_DIR ||\n  process.env.JETBRAINS_NEXT_SOURCE_DIR ||\n  null;\n\nconst JETBRAINS_CHANNELS = [\n  {\n    name: \"jetbrains-stable\",\n    indexUrl:\n      process.env.JETBRAINS_INDEX_URL ||\n      \"https://exafunction.github.io/public/changelog/jetbrains/jetbrains_changelogs.json\",\n    sourceDir: JETBRAINS_SOURCE_DIR,\n    notesDir: path.join(PLUGINS_DIR, \"releases\", \"jetbrains-stable\"),\n    notesImportBase: \"./releases/jetbrains-stable\",\n    changelogPath: path.join(PLUGINS_DIR, \"changelog.mdx\"),\n    changelogTitle: \"Changelog\",\n    changelogDescription:\n      \"Release notes for the Windsurf JetBrains plugin.\",\n  },\n  {\n    name: \"jetbrains-next\",\n    indexUrl:\n      process.env.JETBRAINS_NEXT_INDEX_URL ||\n      \"https://exafunction.github.io/public/changelog/jetbrains-prerelease/jetbrains-prerelease_changelogs.json\",\n    sourceDir: JETBRAINS_SOURCE_DIR,\n    notesDir: path.join(PLUGINS_DIR, \"releases\", \"jetbrains-next\"),\n    notesImportBase: \"./releases/jetbrains-next\",\n    changelogPath: path.join(PLUGINS_DIR, \"changelog-next.mdx\"),\n    changelogTitle: \"Changelog (Pre-release)\",\n    changelogDescription:\n      \"Release notes for the Windsurf JetBrains plugin pre-release builds.\",\n  },\n];\n\nfunction frontmatter(title, description, icon, { rss = false } = {}) {\n  const rssLine = rss ? \"rss: true\\n\" : \"\";\n  return `---\ntitle: \"${title}\"\ndescription: \"${description}\"\nicon: \"${icon}\"\npublic: true\n${rssLine}---\n`;\n}\n\n// Next versions look like \"2.3.1015+next.c46c49e94b\"; strip the channel suffix\n// to get the displayable, sortable version (e.g. \"2.3.1015\").\nfunction cleanVersion(version) {\n  return version.split(/-next|\\+next/)[0];\n}\n\n// Converts an API platform key (e.g. \"darwin-arm64-dmg\") into the camelCase prop\n// name passed to \u003cRelease\u003e (e.g. \"darwinArm64Dmg\").\nfunction platformProp(platformKey) {\n  return platformKey.replace(/-([a-z0-9])/g, (_, char) =\u003e char.toUpperCase());\n}\n\n// Builds the per-OS download attributes (e.g. ` darwinArm64=\"https://...\"`) for a\n// version from its manifest, one per platform that exposes a download URL.\nfunction renderDownloadAttributes(manifest) {\n  return Object.entries(manifest || {})\n    .filter(([, entry]) =\u003e entry \u0026\u0026 typeof entry.url === \"string\")\n    .map(([platformKey, entry]) =\u003e `${platformProp(platformKey)}=\"${entry.url}\"`)\n    .map((attribute) =\u003e ` ${attribute}`)\n    .join(\"\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Picks a representative release date for a version from its per-platform\n * manifests. Each platform entry carries a millisecond epoch `timestamp`; we\n * use the most recent one as the version's release date.\n */\nfunction releaseDateFor(manifest) {\n  const timestamps = Object.values(manifest || {})\n    .map((entry) =\u003e entry \u0026\u0026 entry.timestamp)\n    .filter((ts) =\u003e typeof ts === \"number\" \u0026\u0026 ts \u003e 0);\n\n  if (timestamps.length === 0) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return new Date(Math.max(...timestamps));\n}\n\nfunction formatDate(date) {\n  return date.toLocaleDateString(\"en-US\", {\n    year: \"numeric\",\n    month: \"long\",\n    day: \"numeric\",\n    timeZone: \"UTC\",\n  });\n}\n\nfunction updateOpen(version, manifest) {\n  const date = releaseDateFor(manifest);\n  const description = date ? ` description=\"${formatDate(date)}\"` : \"\";\n  return `\u003cUpdate label=\"v${version}\"${description}\u003e`;\n}\n\n// A version such as \"2.3.15\" maps to a valid MDX/JS component identifier like\n// \"Release_2_3_15\". Returns null when no release note exists for the version.\nfunction snippetComponentName(channel, version) {\n  if (!fs.existsSync(path.join(channel.notesDir, `${version}.md`))) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return `Release_${version.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]+/g, \"_\")}`;\n}\n\n// Changelog entry: release note text plus a collapsible per-OS download block.\n// The newest release (isLatest) gets an open-by-default accordion so it stands\n// out from the rest of the history.\nfunction renderChangelogUpdate(channel, version, manifest, isLatest) {\n  const componentName = snippetComponentName(channel, version);\n  const note = componentName ? `\u003c${componentName} /\u003e` : \"A release was made.\";\n  const lines = [updateOpen(version, manifest), note];\n  const downloads = renderDownloadAttributes(manifest);\n  if (downloads) {\n    const accordionOpen = isLatest ? \" defaultOpen\" : \"\";\n    lines.push(\n      `\u003cAccordion title=\"Download ${version}\"${accordionOpen}\u003e`,\n      `\u003cRelease${downloads} /\u003e`,\n      `\u003c/Accordion\u003e`\n    );\n  }\n  lines.push(`\u003c/Update\u003e`);\n  return lines.join(\"\\n\");\n}\n\n// Releases entry: per-OS download buttons only.\nfunction renderReleaseUpdate(version, manifest) {\n  const downloads = renderDownloadAttributes(manifest);\n  return [\n    updateOpen(version, manifest),\n    `\u003cRelease${downloads} /\u003e`,\n    `\u003c/Update\u003e`,\n  ].join(\"\\n\");\n}\n\n// Compares two dotted numeric versions (e.g. \"2.3.15\" vs \"1.9600.41\") so they\n// can be sorted newest-first. Each segment is compared numerically; shorter\n// versions sort before longer ones when otherwise equal.\nfunction compareVersionsDesc(a, b) {\n  const segmentsA = a.split(\".\").map(Number);\n  const segmentsB = b.split(\".\").map(Number);\n  const length = Math.max(segmentsA.length, segmentsB.length);\n  for (let i = 0; i \u003c length; i++) {\n    const diff = (segmentsB[i] || 0) - (segmentsA[i] || 0);\n    if (diff !== 0) {\n      return diff;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\nfunction renderImport(channel, version) {\n  const componentName = snippetComponentName(channel, version);\n  if (!componentName) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return `import ${componentName} from \"${channel.notesImportBase}/${version}.md\";`;\n}\n\nasync function fetchVersions(apiUrl) {\n  const res = await fetch(apiUrl);\n  if (!res.ok) {\n    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch ${apiUrl}: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`);\n  }\n  const versions = await res.json();\n  if (!Array.isArray(versions)) {\n Returns the parsed fields and the\n// remaining markdown body.\nfunction parseFrontmatter(source) {\n  const match = source.match(/^---\\n([\\s\\S]*?)\\n---\\n?/);\n  if (!match) {\n    return { data: {}, body: source };\n  }\n  const data = {};\n  for (const line of match[1].split(\"\\n\")) {\n    const pair = line.match(/^([A-Za-z0-9_]+):\\s*(.*)$/);\n    if (pair) {\n      data[pair[1]] = pair[2].trim().replace(/^['\"]|['\"]$/g, \"\");\n    }\n  }\n  return { data, body: source.slice(match[0].length) };\n}\n\n// Resolves a CDN note URL to either its local checkout path (when sourceDir is\n// set) or returns the URL to be fetched.\nasync function readNoteSource(channel, noteUrl) {\n  if (channel.sourceDir) {\n    const relative = noteUrl.split(\"/public/changelog/\")[1];\n    return fs.readFileSync(path.join(channel.sourceDir, relative), \"utf8\");\n  }\n  const res = await fetch(noteUrl);\n  if (!res.ok) {\n    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch ${noteUrl}: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`);\n  }\n  return res.text();\n}\n\nasync function readJetbrainsIndex(channel) {\n  if (channel.sourceDir) {\n    const relative = channel.indexUrl.split(\"/public/changelog/\")[1];\n    return JSON.parse(\n      fs.readFileSync(path.join(channel.sourceDir, relative), \"utf8\")\n    );\n  }\n  const res = await fetch(channel.indexUrl);\n  if (!res.ok) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Failed to fetch ${channel.indexUrl}: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`\n    );\n  }\n  return res.json();\n}\n\n// Derives a version from a note URL when its frontmatter omits one.
